text: In this sterile space, with glass reflections and the geometry of walls and pipes, I feel alienated yet intellectually alert. The humming of lights above makes me aware of the layered separation between observer and observed. The next room, with its R+D display and philosophical text, invites reflection on consciousness, technology, and the act of observing or being observed. There’s both intrigue and discomfort—a sense of being caught in the act of inquiry, of existence made visible and strange. I am thoughtful, unsettled, and quietly curious.
